title: Twenty-Six Political and Other Poems (Including 'Petty Job') from the Oxford MSS. Digby 102 and Douce 322
summary: The pinned General Corpus XML is a supported repository copy of University of Michigan encoding APE9594.0001.001, which encodes Josef Kail's 1904 EETS Part I selection. Kail transcribes Digby 102 for works 1-24 and Douce 322 separately for Pety Job and The Bird with Four Feathers. The shared artifact-to-encoding-to-edition stem is classified once, followed by three separately scoped transcription branches. Modern editions, collateral witnesses, catalog descriptions, print scans, manuscript-image objects, request routes, and rights statements remain supporting layers.

entities.label: General Corpus pinned APE9594 artifact
entities.description: The checked-in CME-derived XML repository representation at pinned submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME encoding APE9594.0001.001
entities.description: University of Michigan's 2006 uncorrected OCR encoding at TEI in Libraries Level 1. It contains all twenty-six poem texts and page milestones for omitted front/back matter.
notes: The publicly exposed U-M text is not the same thing as a retrieved raw U-M XML/TIFF package; the corresponding Internet Archive item is dark.

entities.label: Josef Kail, Twenty-Six Political and Other Poems, Part I (1904)
entities.description: EETS Original Series 124, labeled Part I. All twenty-six target poems occupy printed pp. 1-149; most front matter and the glossary/back matter are not textually retained in the U-M encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Josef Kail, ed., Twenty-Six Political and Other Poems (Including 'Petty Job') from the Oxford MSS. Digby 102 and Douce 322, Part I, Early English Text Society Original Series 124 (London: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co., 1904).
bibliographic.title: Twenty-Six Political and Other Poems (Including 'Petty Job') from the Oxford MSS. Digby 102 and Douce 322
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society Original Series 124

notes: No Part II publication or definitive abandonment was verified; this entity is the inspected Part I volume.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Digby 102
entities.description: Parchment manuscript of 142 leaves, produced in London in 1425-1450; the twenty-four target poems occupy ff. 98r-127v and were copied by the Brewers' Guild clerk.

notes: The target sequence is ff. 98r-127v.
The Malvern-area dialect localization describes the scribal language and does not prove composition there.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Douce 322
entities.description: Parchment manuscript produced in England in 1450-1475 and associated with Dartford Dominican priory. Pety Job occupies ff. 10r-15r and The Bird with Four Feathers ff. 15r-16v; both share f. 15r.

notes: The direct f. 15r image shows the Pety Job ending and colophon followed by the decorated opening of The Bird with Four Feathers.
DIMEV 3075's ff. 10ra-15vb endpoint is retained as a conflicting source claim rather than silently repaired or treated as a second foliation.

entities.label: Internet Archive Toronto scan of Kail's 1904 Part I volume
entities.description: Complete 256-page scan of the inspected published Part I carrier. The target poems are printed pp. 1-149 / PDF pp. 39-187. This is a print-volume facsimile, not a manuscript facsimile.

entities.label: Internet Archive OCR XML derivative of the Kail 1904 Toronto scan
entities.description: Complete DjVu OCR XML for the scanned printed volume. Provider association is clear, but it is not asserted as the source of the earlier U-M encoding.
notes: A conservative version relation records association with the scan without claiming an unsupported production direction into U-M.

entities.label: Internet Archive scandata and metadata for the Kail 1904 Toronto scan
entities.description: Page/leaf sequencing and file metadata for the print-volume scan; a delivery/description layer rather than target text or manuscript imagery.

entities.label: Internet Archive metadata stub for dark U-M item ape9594.0001.001.umich.edu
entities.description: The extant metadata response reports is_dark=true and exposes no public package files. The underlying raw U-M XML/TIFF package is unavailable and its current survival or recoverability through another authorized route is unknown.

entities.label: Pety Job abstract work
entities.description: Schema workaround for the anonymous mid-fifteenth-century penitential poem. This conceptual node is not a material catalog record, carrier, or witness.
notes: Use of catalog_description is provisional because the lineage schema has no abstract-work class.

entities.label: The Bird with Four Feathers abstract work
entities.description: Schema workaround for the anonymous penitential allegory. This conceptual node is not a material catalog record, carrier, or witness.
notes: Use of catalog_description is provisional because the lineage schema has no abstract-work class.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38
entities.description: Supporting Pety Job witness at ff. 6r-10r. Physical and imaging access are requestable; no current public whole-object facsimile was verified.

entities.label: Magdalene College, Pepys MS 1584
entities.description: Supporting Pety Job witness at ff. 48r-62r; METS prints 48a-62a, where a means recto. Advance research and photographic-service enquiry are required.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.21 (James 601)
entities.description: Fifteenth-century paper miscellany of 320 folios. The Bird with Four Feathers occupies ff. 34r-37v and Pety Job ff. 38r-50v.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1706
entities.description: Supporting witness: Pety Job ff. 11r-15v and The Bird with Four Feathers ff. 16r-17v. The manuscript has 216 folios and is dated 1474-1524; the first part is circa 1480-1500.
notes: Likely copied from Douce 322 or from a common exemplar; the exact relationship remains qualified.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Bodley 596
entities.description: Complete supporting witness of The Bird with Four Feathers at ff. 21v-24v. The current summary catalog's containing item reaches f. 25, a distinct source-level boundary.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Lat. misc. e. 85
entities.description: Supporting Bird fragment in sixteenth-century English paper part 2. Current catalog item locus is inferred ff. 79-80; DIMEV gives ff. 79-81 and says the same hand recopies the first nine lines at f. 82v.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS O.9.38 (James 1450)
entities.description: Fifteenth/sixteenth-century Glastonbury commonplace book on paper. It carries an altered/abridged Bird witness at ff. 24r-25r; DIMEV's ff. 24-25 is a coarser locator.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 18 A X
entities.description: Incomplete supporting Bird witness at ff. 119v-123 in a fifteenth-century theological and verse miscellany.

entities.label: Stonyhurst Boardman MS XXIII (23)
entities.description: Incomplete supporting Bird witness. DIMEV cites f. 60v; the current official catalog identifies the work at ff. 60v-63v, 201 lines, ending imperfectly.

entities.label: Huntington Library HM 906
entities.description: Supporting prosaicized Bird lines 1-3 fragment at f. 60v in a 164-leaf parchment statute book.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian one-canvas selection from MS Digby 102
entities.description: Complete capture of the provider's single exposed image, fol. 11v. The target ff. 98r-127v are absent; this is not a complete codex facsimile.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian four-canvas selection from MS Douce 322
entities.description: Complete capture of four provider-selected slides. Canvas 1, fol. 15r, contains the Pety Job ending/colophon and Bird opening; the other three canvases are unrelated.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian thirteen-canvas selection from MS Bodley 596
entities.description: Complete capture of the provider's thirteen selected canvases; none depicts target ff. 21v-24v.

entities.label: Trinity MS R.3.21 686-canvas provider IIIF object
entities.description: Complete relative to all 686 provider-published canvases. Bird is on canvases 73-80 / ff. 34r-37v and Pety Job on 81-106 / ff. 38r-50v. Completeness does not independently prove every physical codex surface.

entities.label: Trinity MS O.9.38 99-opening provider IIIF object
entities.description: Distinct paired-opening object with 99 canvases including front/back covers. Bird appears on canvases 31-32 / ff. 24r-25r; canvas 33 begins the next text on f. 25v.
notes: This object is not interchangeable with the 196-canvas CUDL/Scriptorium single-surface object.

entities.label: CUDL/Scriptorium MS O.9.38 196-canvas IIIF object
entities.description: Distinct single-surface object with 196 canvases. Bird appears on canvases 61-63 = ff. 24r-25r; canvas 64 = f. 25v begins the next text.
notes: The exact manifest license field is null; component-specific attribution terms are recorded separately from the 99-opening object's CC BY-NC 4.0 statement.

entities.label: CUDL/Scriptorium TEI description for Trinity MS O.9.38
entities.description: Current descriptive TEI; msItem 21 gives locus 24r-25r, Middle English main language, Latin title, incipit on 24r, and explicit/final rubric on 25r. It is metadata, not a target-text or image reproduction.

entities.label: Huntington HM 906 four-image Digital Scriptorium selection
entities.description: Complete relative to the provider's four exposed images, but partial to the codex. The target f. 60v is absent.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38 printed facsimile (1979)
entities.description: Complete modern printed manuscript facsimile with preliminary matter and 261 reproduced folios. Purchase/library access is verified; no public digital copy or local cache was found.

bibliographic.citation: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38, introduction by Frances McSparran and P. R. Robinson (London: Scolar Press, 1979).

entities.label: PIMS 2025 microfilm checklist entries for Ff.II.38 and Pepys 1584
entities.description: The checklist names two requestable microfilms without folio ranges, completeness statements, or DIGITAL SCAN/COPY labels. The cached checklist itself contains no target text or images.

entities.label: PIMS microfilm of CUL MS Ff.II.38
entities.description: Uncached requestable microfilm listed without a folio range. Target presence and physical-manuscript coverage remain unknown pending PIMS confirmation.

entities.label: PIMS microfilm of Pepys MS 1584
entities.description: Uncached requestable microfilm listed without a folio range. Target presence and physical-manuscript coverage remain unknown pending PIMS confirmation.

entities.label: Susanna Greer Fein, Moral Love Songs and Laments (1998)
entities.description: Complete modern edition of Pety Job and The Bird with Four Feathers with the relevant witnesses collated; supporting and not an ancestor of Kail 1904.

bibliographic.citation: Susanna Greer Fein, ed., Moral Love Songs and Laments (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 1998).
bibliographic.title: Moral Love Songs and Laments

entities.label: Helen Barr, The Digby Poems (2009)
entities.description: Complete modern edition freshly transcribed from Digby 102 ff. 98r-127v with critical apparatus. Purchase or library access is verified; it is not an ancestor of Kail 1904.

bibliographic.citation: Helen Barr, ed., The Digby Poems: A New Edition of the Lyrics (Exeter: University of Exeter Press, 2009).
bibliographic.title: The Digby Poems: A New Edition of the Lyrics

entities.label: Louis J. P. Verheij, Where of is mad al mankynde (2009)
entities.description: Complete modern critical edition and doctoral study of all twenty-four Digby poems; supporting and independent of the repository's 1904 source chain.

bibliographic.citation: Louis J. P. Verheij, Where of is mad al mankynde: The Middle English Digby Poems (doctoral thesis, Leiden University, 2009).
bibliographic.title: Where of is mad al mankynde

entities.label: Louis J. P. Verheij, New Light on the Author of the Twenty-Four Poems (2012)
entities.description: Supporting scholarly argument rejecting Kail's author profile and proposing, without identification, a Westminster Benedictine/Chancery-clerk profile. It contains scattered quotations but is not a target edition.

bibliographic.citation: Louis J. P. Verheij, New Light on the Author of the Twenty-Four Poems in Oxford, Bodleian Library, Digby 102, Neophilologus (2012), DOI 10.1007/s11061-011-9292-y.

notes: catalog_description is used because the schema has no scholarly-study entity type.

entities.label: Cathy Hume, The Life of Job: Translation, Poem or Play? (2018)
entities.description: Supporting study of the Pety Job textual/liturgical family with a short quotation of lines 1-8; not a complete critical edition.

bibliographic.citation: Cathy Hume, The Life of Job: Translation, Poem or Play? (2018).

notes: catalog_description is used because the schema has no scholarly-study entity type.

entities.label: DIMEV record 924 for The Bird with Four Feathers

entities.label: PCMEP record for The Bird with Four Feathers

primary_transmission_paths.label: Shared repository-to-edition stem
primary_transmission_paths.description: The shared provenance stem is recorded once. copied_from expresses supported repository provenance without byte/canonical equality, followed by the U-M encoding's direct dependence on Kail 1904.

primary_transmission_paths.label: Digby 102 branch for works I-XXIV
primary_transmission_paths.description: Kail's direct transcription branch for the first twenty-four works, Digby 102 ff. 98r-127v.

primary_transmission_paths.label: Douce 322 Pety Job branch
primary_transmission_paths.description: Kail's separately scoped direct transcription of Pety Job, Douce 322 ff. 10r-15r.

primary_transmission_paths.label: Douce 322 Bird branch
primary_transmission_paths.description: Kail's separately scoped direct transcription of The Bird with Four Feathers, Douce 322 ff. 15r-16v; f. 15r is shared with the Pety Job branch.

supporting_relationships.label: Printed-volume scan and digital derivatives
supporting_relationships.description: Separates the printed-volume facsimile, associated OCR/scandata, and dark U-M package metadata from the historical manuscript chain.

supporting_relationships.label: Pety Job and Bird work/witness census
supporting_relationships.description: Keeps the two abstract-work workaround nodes and every complete, altered, incomplete, or fragmentary supporting witness outside the Kail/U-M primary production path.

supporting_relationships.label: Manuscript facsimiles, microfilms, and descriptions
supporting_relationships.description: Preserves selected-image, complete-provider-object, printed-facsimile, unknown-extent microfilm, and catalog-description layers with exact coverage qualifications.

supporting_relationships.label: Modern Digby editions and authorship study
supporting_relationships.description: Modern editions and scholarship are supporting, not ancestors of Kail 1904.

supporting_relationships.label: Fein's Pety Job edition and collation
supporting_relationships.description: Fein's complete modern Pety Job text uses Douce as base and collates the other four census witnesses.

supporting_relationships.label: Fein's Bird edition and collation
supporting_relationships.description: Fein's complete modern Bird text uses Bodley 596 as base and collates the other complete, altered, incomplete, and fragmentary witnesses.

local_copies.label: CUL Image Supply and Reproduction guidance
notes: Cached object has zero target-text or target-image overlap; source-context note: Official imaging and permissions request guidance. Links the current online form and separates copy supply, permission, and publication/commercial-use request purposes; contains no manuscript images.

local_copies.label: CUL two-letter manuscripts access page
notes: Cached object has zero target-text or target-image overlap; source-context note: Official class-level catalog and physical-access guidance. Identifies the two-letter class, printed catalog basis, conservation qualification, and Special Collections contacts; contains no manuscript images.
Public policy and access guidance; this access object is not the manuscript or an image supply itself.

local_copies.label: Cambridge Digital Library exact-shelfmark query for MS Ff.2.38
notes: Cached object has zero target-text or target-image overlap; source-context note: Exact documentShelfLocator query for the manuscript. Cached JSON has response.numFound = 0 and zero documents; this is negative-search evidence, not a facsimile or a claim that no internal images exist.
Exact current query returns zero documents; this dated result does not prove that internal or historical photography is absent.

local_copies.label: Pepys Library catalogues and research tools guide
notes: Cached object has zero target-text or target-image overlap; source-context note: Current two-page official catalog guide. States that there is no online Pepys Library catalog and directs manuscript researchers to printed catalogue volume V.i; contains no target manuscript images.

local_copies.label: Pepys Library guidelines for readers
notes: Cached object has zero target-text or target-image overlap; source-context note: Current one-page official reader rules. Prohibits reader photography and directs researchers to the College photographic service; contains no target manuscript images.

local_copies.label: Pepys Library information for researchers
notes: Cached object has zero target-text or target-image overlap; source-context note: Official appointment, photographic-service, email, and telephone route. Current page directs photographic-services and research enquiries to the Pepys Librarian; contains no target manuscript images.
Catalog and reader guidance only; contains no target manuscript images.

local_copies.label: Pepys Digital Library manuscript inventory
notes: Cached object has zero target-text or target-image overlap; source-context note: Complete current official list of Pepys manuscript objects published on the page. Lists MSS 878, 911, 1047, 1236, 1307, 1661, and part of 2981, not MS 1584; this is negative inventory evidence, not proof that internal images do not exist.
Current inventory omits MS 1584; this is a dated negative finding.

local_copies.label: PIMS Microfilm Collection checklist 2025
notes: The cached checklist contains no target text or manuscript images; it only names two microfilm holdings. Whether either uncached microfilm contains the target and how much of each manuscript it covers remain unknown pending PIMS confirmation. Checklist names both films without folio ranges or DIGITAL SCAN/COPY labels; target presence and whole-object completeness require provider confirmation; reproduction requires consent from the original repository.
The checklist itself has no target overlap. The two uncached microfilms retain unknown target presence and extent.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Kail's published Part I volume contains title and introductory matter, twenty-six poems on printed pages 1-149, a glossary, and other back matter.
editorial_practices.representation: The U-M encoding presents all twenty-six poem divisions and retains page milestones across the carrier while omitting the prose of most front matter and the glossary/back matter.
editorial_practices.description: The electronic object is complete for its encoded twenty-six-work selection, not a transcription of every page of the printed volume and not a claim about the completion of Kail's projected multipart publication.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The target anthology combines twenty-four poems from Bodleian MS Digby 102 with Pety Job and The Bird with Four Feathers from Bodleian MS Douce 322.
editorial_practices.representation: Kail prints the twenty-four Digby works first and the two Douce works as items XXV and XXVI in one separately titled Part I volume.
editorial_practices.description: This editorial selection is the work-level unit encoded by U-M; it is neither either whole manuscript nor a single anonymous medieval work.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Proofs of the twenty-six transcriptions could be checked against Digby 102 and Douce 322.
editorial_practices.representation: Kail credits Miss A. F. Parker with reading the proofs against the manuscripts.
editorial_practices.description: The credit establishes manuscript proof-collation for the edition but does not establish Parker's expanded identity or turn supporting modern editions into Kail's sources.

editorial_practices.source_feature: The manuscript transcriptions required modern editorial headlines, some side-notes, punctuation, and hyphenation for print.
editorial_practices.representation: Kail credits Frederick James Furnivall with supplying headlines and some side-notes, adding punctuation and hyphens, and supervising the press sheets.
editorial_practices.description: This record keeps Furnivall's stated interventions distinct from Kail's principal editorship and from the medieval wording of the witnesses.
editorial_practices.scope: The twenty-six-poem edited selection and its editorial presentation

evidence.summary: Bibliographic evidence for a complete modern edition freshly transcribed from Digby 102 ff. 98r-127v with critical apparatus; purchasable/library access only.

evidence.citation: Louis J. P. Verheij, Where of is mad al mankynde (doctoral thesis, 2009).
evidence.summary: Complete modern critical edition of the twenty-four Digby poems; broad chronology, Malvern-area scribal dialect, and author-profile analysis.

evidence.citation: Louis J. P. Verheij, New Light on the Author of the Twenty-Four Poems (2012).
evidence.summary: Rejects Kail's abbot/prior/parliament profile and argues a Westminster Benedictine/Chancery-clerk profile without identifying the poet.

evidence.citation: Cathy Hume, The Life of Job: Translation, Poem or Play? (2018), accepted manuscript.
evidence.summary: Pety Job textual/liturgical family, Latin-English presentation, and quotation of lines 1-8; not a complete target edition.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library two-letter manuscripts access and Image Supply guidance.
evidence.summary: Class-level physical-access guidance, conservation qualifications, current Image Supply route, and separation of copy supply, permission, publication, and commercial-use requests.

evidence.citation: CUDL exact documentShelfLocator query for Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38.
evidence.summary: Current API response has numFound 0 and zero documents. This is dated negative-search evidence, not proof of no internal or historical imagery.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38, introduction by Frances McSparran and P. R. Robinson (Scolar Press, 1979).
evidence.summary: Bibliographic evidence for a complete 1979 printed facsimile with preliminary matter and 261 reproduced folios; no eBook/public digital copy verified.

evidence.citation: Pepys Library information for researchers, catalog guide, and reader guidelines.
evidence.summary: Advance appointment and photographic-service route; no online catalog; reader photography prohibited; supplied-image terms require confirmation.

evidence.citation: Pepys Digital Library current manuscript inventory.
evidence.summary: The complete current published list names MSS 878, 911, 1047, 1236, 1307, 1661, and part of 2981, not MS 1584.

evidence.citation: PIMS Microfilm Collection checklist 2025.
evidence.summary: Entries name CUL Ff.II.38 and Pepys 1584 without folio ranges or DIGITAL SCAN/COPY labels; target presence and extent of both uncached films remain unknown. Reproduction requires original-repository consent.
evidence.locator: Pepys 1584 printed p. 28/PDF p. 31; Ff.II.38 printed p. 49/PDF p. 52

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalog, Harley MS 1706.
evidence.summary: Current shelfmark, extent, date, target loci, and currently unavailable legacy digital-images route.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts catalog, Royal MS 18 A X.
evidence.summary: Current shelfmark, fifteenth-century miscellany context, incomplete Bird witness ff. 119v-123, and no current public image object.

evidence.citation: British Library physical-consultation and Reference Services forms.
evidence.summary: The Office form is for physical consultation; the LibAnswers form is for catalog/access and research-reference questions. Neither is an imaging, reproduction, or copies-order route.

evidence.citation: Catalogue of the Boardman Collection of Mediaeval Manuscripts at Stonyhurst (2020).
evidence.summary: Complete official catalog. Boardman MS XXIII item identifies The Four Feathers at ff. 60v-63v, 201 lines, ending imperfectly; current Archives/contact route.

evidence.citation: Huntington Library digital catalog record for HM 906.
evidence.summary: Official shelfmark, 164-leaf parchment statute-book description, partial-scan note, and Aeon imaging route; DIMEV/Fein locate the prosaicized lines 1-3 at f. 60v.

evidence.citation: Huntington HM 906 four-canvas IIIF object.
evidence.summary: All four provider-selected canvases are cached; target f. 60v is absent. Manifest has no license field and links to Rights and Permissions.

evidence.citation: Huntington Imaging Services guide and 2026 price list.
evidence.summary: Aeon Imaging Request procedure, formats, per-scan prices, delivery, rush, and large-order qualifications; conditional rights guidance does not itself establish HM 906 public-domain status or a blanket image license.
notes: The exact 2026 price list is a separately checksummed local copy on the same policy access.

open_questions.question: Can an authorized raw U-M XML/TIFF package be recovered, and what transformations distinguish it from the repository XML?
next_steps: Ask U-M for item-level production files or transformation documentation.
If raw XML becomes available, compare bytes and canonical XML before making any equality claim.
Inventory any supplied TIFF set independently rather than inferring 246 images from page-break milestones.
notes: The current copied_from relation records supported provenance only; the Internet Archive package is dark and exposes no files.

open_questions.question: Was a Part II of Kail's projected publication ever published or definitively abandoned?
next_steps: Search EETS publication records, library catalogs, and contemporary notices for a continuation.
Retain Part I in the edition title until positive evidence resolves the projected publication.
notes: The cached scanned volume is complete for the published 1904 Part I carrier and includes all twenty-six target poems; that does not establish the fate of a projected Part II.

open_questions.question: What are Josef Kail's authoritative full life dates, and what do they imply for editorial matter outside the United States?
next_steps: Locate an authoritative biographical or civil record that establishes Kail's death date.
Reassess jurisdiction-specific term calculations only after the identity and dates are verified.
notes: GND verifies Kail's preferred name and identifiers but supplies no birth or death date; the manifest therefore asserts United States publication-date status separately from unknown worldwide status.

open_questions.question: How should DIMEV 3075's printed ff. 10ra-15vb locator be reconciled with the directly inspected ending and colophon of Pety Job on Douce 322 f. 15r?
next_steps: Ask DIMEV whether 15vb is a legacy locator, typographical error, or a distinct foliation convention.
Preserve both source-attributed claims and the shared f. 15r seam until resolved.

open_questions.question: Why does PCMEP report 241 lines for The Bird with Four Feathers while Kail and the repository XML contain 240 verse lines?
next_steps: Collate the PCMEP/Brown base text against Kail and the XML.
Keep 241 as a source-specific count rather than changing the audited repository extent.

open_questions.question: Are either of the provider-published Trinity O.9.38 digital objects coterminous with every physical codex surface?
next_steps: Request an object-level inventory or digitization statement from Trinity/CUDL.
Continue to describe completeness only relative to each exposed provider canvas inventory.
notes: The 99-opening Trinity object and the separate 196-canvas CUDL/Scriptorium object are distinct inventories; their different canvas numbers do not conflict.

open_questions.question: Can complete current digital facsimiles be obtained for the two base manuscripts, Digby 102 and Douce 322?
next_steps: Request quotations for unavailable portions through the Bodleian mediated-copying and Imaging Services routes.
Keep selected-image Digital Bodleian objects distinct from any newly supplied reproduction and its terms.

open_questions.question: What are the physical extents and target-work coverage of the uncached PIMS microfilms for CUL Ff.II.38 and Pepys 1584?
next_steps: Ask PIMS for reel extents, frame counts, target folio presence, and order conditions.
Obtain original-repository consent before ordering any reproduction that requires it.
notes: The checklist names both manuscripts but supplies neither folio ranges nor DIGITAL SCAN/COPY labels, so extent and target presence remain unknown.

open_questions.question: Can public or repository-supplied whole-object facsimiles be obtained for Pepys 1584, Harley 1706, Royal 18 A X, Bodl. Lat. misc. e. 85, Stonyhurst 23, and HM 906?
next_steps: Ask each holding institution for whole-object feasibility, cost, delivery, conservation limits, and supplied-image terms.
Treat Huntington's four-image object as an explicitly partial sample whose target f. 60v is absent.
Use the British Library Office form only for physical consultation and Reference Services only for reference questions unless an imaging route is separately verified.

open_questions.question: What exact reuse licenses govern the current DIMEV HTML, PCMEP page, Bodleian catalog XML, Fein PDF, Verheij thesis, and Hume accepted manuscript?
next_steps: Locate exact resource-level license statements or obtain written permission before redistributing source files.
Keep public access and open download separate from copyright and reuse permission.
notes: No blanket medieval-text public-domain inference is applied to modern catalogs, editions, scholarship, metadata, or digitized images.

open_questions.question: What additional permissions, if any, are required to publish or commercially reuse exact Trinity and Bodleian images under their layered object statements and service terms?
next_steps: Present the exact intended use to Trinity and Bodleian and request written guidance.
Evaluate each supplied or downloaded image under its own object license, service terms, and publication-permission layer.

review_notes: The five reviewed primary relations are classified exactly once: one two-edge shared stem and three one-edge transcription branches.
The two Douce branches share fol. 15r at the exact Pety Job/Bird transition.
The General Corpus artifact-to-U-M copied_from edge records supported provenance only; the dark U-M raw package prevents any byte-for-byte or canonical-equality claim.
All other witnesses and modern editions are supporting relationships, not ancestors of Kail's 1904 text unless an explicit relation says otherwise.
Printed-volume scans are not manuscript facsimiles.
Complete IIIF bundles are complete only relative to the provider-published canvas inventory, not automatically every physical codex surface.
The Trinity 99-opening O.9.38 object and the distinct 196-canvas CUDL/Scriptorium object retain separate identities, inventories, target locators, access records, and rights statements.
Public access, copyright/license assertions, repository contract terms, and publication permission are separate dimensions.
No exact manuscript text receives a global public-domain inference merely from medieval date.
